challenges.title: Basic CSS Counters
challenges.description: Write CSS that uses `counter-reset` and `counter-increment` to create an automatic section numbering system. On the `body` selector, use `counter-reset: section`. On the `h2` selector, use `counter-increment: section`. On the `h2::before` pseudo-element, use `content` with `counter(section)` to display the number followed by a period and space. Assign your CSS to the variable `css`.
challenges.test_code:
assert 'counter-reset' in css.lower(), 'Should use counter-reset to initialize the counter'
assert 'counter-increment' in css.lower(), 'Should use counter-increment to advance the counter'
assert 'content' in css.lower(), 'Should use content property to display the counter'
assert 'counter(' in css.lower(), 'Should use counter() function'
assert '::before' in css.lower(), 'Should use ::before pseudo-element'
hints: counter-reset initializes a counter; counter-increment advances it by 1
Use counter(name) inside the content property to display the counter value

challenges.title: Nested Counters
challenges.description: Write CSS that creates nested counters for a multi-level numbering system (e.g., 1.1, 1.2, 2.1). On the `ol` selector, use `counter-reset: item`. On the `li` selector, use `counter-increment: item`. On the `li::before` pseudo-element, use `content` with the `counters(item, ".")` function (note: `counters` with an 's') to display hierarchical numbering with a dot separator. Also set the `li` selector to have `list-style-type: none`. Assign your CSS to the variable `css`.
challenges.test_code:
assert 'counter-reset' in css.lower(), 'Should use counter-reset on the list'
assert 'counter-increment' in css.lower(), 'Should use counter-increment on list items'
assert 'counters(' in css.lower(), 'Should use counters() function for nested numbering'
assert 'content' in css.lower(), 'Should use content property to display counters'
assert 'list-style-type' in css.lower(), 'Should set list-style-type'
assert 'none' in css.lower(), 'Should remove default list markers'
hints: counters() (with an 's') concatenates all counters of the same name with a separator
Each nested ol resets its own counter, creating the hierarchy automatically
